concepts.title: Biodiversity Hotspots
concepts.description: Biodiversity hotspots are regions with high levels of endemism and are threatened by human activities. They cover 2.3% of the Earth's surface but are home to 50-80% of the planet's species.

concepts.title: Role of Individuals in Environmental Conservation
concepts.description: Individuals can contribute to environmental conservation by adopting simple practices such as reducing, reusing, and recycling waste, conserving energy and water, and using public transport.

concepts.title: Challenges Facing Environmental Conservation
concepts.description: The major challenges facing environmental conservation include population explosion, industrialization, urbanization, and lack of awareness and education among the masses.

concepts.title: 3Rs
concepts.description: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle - a simple mnemonic to remember the key practices for environmental conservation.

concepts.title: Confusing Biodiversity with Endemism
concepts.description: Biodiversity refers to the variety of species in an ecosystem, while endemism refers to species found only in a particular region. Students often confuse the two terms, which can lead to incorrect answers in exams.

concepts.title: The Rio Declaration and Agenda 21
concepts.description: The Rio Declaration and Agenda 21 are key outcomes of the 1992 Earth Summit. The Rio Declaration outlines 27 principles for sustainable development, while Agenda 21 is a comprehensive plan of action for the 21st century.

concepts.title: Global Environmental Governance
concepts.description: The United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) plays a crucial role in global environmental governance, providing a platform for international cooperation and decision-making on environmental issues.

concepts.title: Importance of Sustainable Development
concepts.description: Sustainable development is essential for meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.
